1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Handle no-changes scenario in erk-impl workflow gracefully. 3→ 4→When implementation produces no code changes (duplicate plan, work already merged), 5→this command updates the PR with diagnostic information and marks it ready for review. 6→ 7→This replaces workflow failure with an informational PR that users can review and close. 8→ 9→Usage: 10→ erk exec handle-no-changes \ 11→ --pr-number 123 \ 12→ --plan-id 456 \ 13→ --behind-count 5 \ 14→ --base-branch master \ 15→ [--recent-commits "abc1234 Fix bug\\ndef5678 Add feature"] \ 16→ [--run-url https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/789] 17→ 18→Output: 19→ JSON object with success status 20→ 21→Exit Codes: 22→ 0: Success (PR updated, labeled, and marked ready) 23→ 1: Error (GitHub API failure) 24→""" 25→ 26→import json 27→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass 28→ 29→import click 30→

session.assistant:
Now I understand the issue. The error is "PR #123 not found" when trying to update the lifecycle stage at line 221. The test is passing pr_number=123 but the FakeLocalGitHub was created with pr_details containing only plan_id=456. The command is trying to find PR #123 in the backend but it doesn't exist.
Let me verify this is the actual issue by checking the PlannedPRBackend implementation:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: uv run grep -r "PR .* not found" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src --include="*.py" | head -20
Search for PR not found error message

1→"""Draft PR implementation of plan storage. 2→ 3→Uses GitHub draft pull requests as the backing store for plans. 4→PR body contains the plan-header metadata block followed by plan content. 5→The "erk-plan" label identifies plan PRs vs regular draft PRs. 6→""" 7→ 8→import logging 9→from collections.abc import Mapping 10→from datetime import UTC 11→from pathlib import Path 12→ 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.label_ops import add_labels_resilient 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 17→ add_metadata_block, 18→ find_metadata_block, 19→ has_metadata_block, 20→ render_metadata_block, 21→ replace_metadata_block_in_body, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import ( 24→ format_plan_header_body, 25→) 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 27→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION, 28→ CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL, 29→ LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE, 30→ OBJECTIVE_ISSUE,

400→ ) -> object | PlanNotFound: 401→ """Get a single metadata field from the plan-header block in the PR body. 402→ 403→ Args: 404→ repo_root: Repository root directory 405→ plan_id: PR number as string 406→ field_name: Name of the metadata field to read 407→ 408→ Returns: 409→ Field value (may be None if unset), or PlanNotFound if PR doesn't exist 410→ """ 411→ pr_number = int(plan_id) 412→ result = self._github.get_pr(repo_root, pr_number) 413→ if isinstance(result, PRNotFound): 414→ return PlanNotFound(pr_id=plan_id) 415→ 416→ block = find_metadata_block(result.body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER) 417→ if block is None: 418→ return None 419→ 420→ return block.data.get(field_name) 421→ 422→ def get_all_metadata_fields( 423→ self, 424→ repo_root: Path, 425→ plan_id: str, 426→ ) -> dict[str, object] | PlanNotFound: 427→ """Get all metadata fields from the plan-header block in the PR body. 428→ 429→ Args:
... [175 lines omitted] ...
465→ PlanHeaderNotFoundError: If PR body has no plan-header metadata block 466→ RuntimeError: If PR not found or update fails 472→ raise RuntimeError(msg) 476→ raise PlanHeaderNotFoundError("plan-header block not found in PR body") 516→ RuntimeError: If PR not found 522→ raise RuntimeError(msg) 553→ RuntimeError: If PR not found or update fails 559→ raise RuntimeError(msg) 582→ RuntimeError: If PR not found 588→ raise RuntimeError(msg)
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I understand the issue completely. Looking at line 468-472 of `planned_pr.py`, the `update_metadata` method tries to fetch PR #123 but it doesn't exist in the FakeLocalGitHub's pr_details dictionary. The test creates a FakeLocalGitHub with only PR #456 in its pr_details.
The test failure is at line 221 in handle_no_changes.py where it calls `backend.update_metadata()`. The command expects the PR (impl PR #123) to exist in the GitHub mock, but it doesn't.
## Summary
**Test Failure Found:**
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_handle_no_changes.py`
**Test:** `test_cli_success` (line 190-220)
**Issue:** The test invokes the `handle_no_changes` command with PR number 123, but the FakeLocalGitHub mock is initialized with only PR 456. When the command tries to update the PR's metadata at line 221 of `handle_no_changes.py`, it fails with:
``` "message": "Failed to update lifecycle stage: PR #123 not found" ```
**Root Cause:** At line 192, the test creates GitHub with `_create_github_with_plan_pr(456)` which sets up only PR #456. However, the command is invoked with `--pr-number 123` at line 200. The `PlannedPRBackend.update_metadata()` method (line 468-472 of `planned_pr.py`) tries to fetch PR #123, but it doesn't exist in the mock's `pr_details` dictionary.
**The command flow:** 1. Updates PR title/body (line 186-191) - succeeds because FakeLocalGitHub doesn't validate 2. Adds label (line 203-209) - succeeds 3. Updates metadata lifecycle_stage (line 221-225) - **FAILS** because PR #123 doesn't exist
